name: voacanga africana
mechanism: Alkaloid constituents can modulate neuronal signaling pathways
effects: dreaming or folk medicine used in ritual
safetyNotes: Moderate risk: side effects or medication interactions are plausible. Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician; Do not combine with serotonergic drugs or MAOIs without medical guidance.

sources.title: PubMed search: Voacanga africana
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Voacanga africana is a small tree native to West Africa. Its bark and seeds are traditionally used for spiritual purposes and stimulation, due to the presence of powerful indole alkaloids.
